data.title: Hedge funds going long On Semi, short NXP, Wells Fargo says
data.description:
Research firm Wells Fargo has weighed in on the automotive and industrial-focused part of the semiconductor industry and the overarching view from investors appears to be mixed.

"Investors largely agree w/ our view the auto semi trade is in a slightly easier / better position than industrials given [inventory] digestion across both [markets]," analyst Joe Quatrochi wrote in a note to clients.

Quatrochi also noted that some investors agree with the thesis that there will be long-term content growth in the automotive space, though others have concerns about plateaus coming in 2025, as the mix from high-end vehicles normalizes after the pandemic and the mix from electric vehicles "remains slow ex-China." The sentiment for the industrial part of the market remains negative, but Quatrochi wondered if there could be a bottom in the first quarter of next year.

Trade ideas

Despite the mixed picture for the group, one popular hedge fund trade that has appeared has been to go long On Semiconductor (NASDAQ:ON) and short NXP Semiconductor (NASDAQ:NXPI), Quatrochi disclosed. Those who are bullish about On said the stock has an "undemanding" valuation and there is the opportunity for a recovery in the silicon carbide market next year, while the feedback for NXP has been "largely negative." Concerns about competition, notably from Qualcomm (QCOM), have cropped up, and there is the belief that the company's targets provided at its analyst day are "too lofty."

Other insights from investors include a "passionate" defense of Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR) after last month's sell-off, along with the "generally positive" long-term thesis about the rising benefit from electronic design automation, which should help Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) and to a lesser extent, Synopsys (SNPS). Quatrochi has an Overweight rating on Cadence and an Equal-Weight rating on Synopsys.

Lastly, sentiment is mixed on companies like Texas Instruments (TXN) and Analog Devices (ADI), while feedback was positive on Allegro Microsystems (ALGM), Quatrochi added.

data.title: Madison Square Garden buys back 681.6K shares
data.description:
• Madison Square Garden Entertainment (NYSE:MSGE) said it has bought back 681.6K shares of MSGE Class A common stock at an average price of $36.68 apiece for ~$25 million.
  • The period for these purcahses were from November 20 through December 2.
  • The purcahses were funded using cash on hand.
  • The firm has ~$85 million remaining under its existing share repurchase authorization.

data.title: WESCO International PFD A declares $0.664 dividend
data.description:
• WESCO International PFD A (WCC.PR.A) declares $0.664/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 10.17%
  • Payable Dec. 31; for shareholders of record Dec. 13; ex-div Dec. 13.
  • See WCC.PR.A Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Worthington Steel acquires controlling stake in Italy-based Sitem Group
data.description:
• Worthington Steel (NYSE:WS) announced on Tuesday, that it has reached an agreement to acquire through its subsidiary, Tempel Steel Company, a controlling equity stake in Italy-based Sitem.
  • Sitem Group produces electric motor laminations for automotive and industrial applications in Europe. Worthington Steel will acquire the contribution of Worthington Steel’s Nagold, Germany, facility and the subscription of reserved share capital increases, an approximately 52% stake in Sitem Group with the option to increase ownership in the future.
  • The transaction is expected to close in early 2025.
  • Sitem Group operates six facilities in Europe including Italy, Switzerland, Slovakia and France.
  • Sitem’s leadership, including Chairman Fabrizio Scarca, CEO Marco Bartoloni and Chief Purchasing Officer Gabriella Scarca, will continue to lead the business from the Sitem Group headquarters in Trevi, Italy.
  • Worthington Steel (WS) shares fell 1.60% in pre-market trading on Tuesday.

data.title: Village Super Market GAAP EPS of $0.86, revenue of $557.7M
data.description:
• Village Super Market press release (NASDAQ:VLGEA): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.86.
  • Revenue of $557.7M (+4.0% Y/Y).
  • Sales increased 4.0% and same store sales increased 2.4%
  • Same store digital sales increased 8%

data.title: Rezolute gets FDA orphan drug designation for its hypoglycemia treatment
data.description:
• Rezolute (NASDAQ:RZLT) announced the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has granted Orphan Drug Designation (ODD) to ersodetug for the treatment of hypoglycemia due to tumor HI.
  • The FDA's Orphan Drug Designation is intended to encourage the development of treatments for rare diseases.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Easterly Government Properties acquires three properties
data.description:
• Office REIT Easterly Government Properties (NYSE:DEA) has acquired a 97% leased, combined 295,253 sq. ft. campus across three assets leased primarily to the Wake County Public School System.
  • The three properties serve as multi-purpose facilities, functioning as both operational headquarters and public-facing service centers.
  • Easterly now owns, directly or through joint ventures, 100 properties totaling 9.8M leased sq. ft.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Allied Corp signs supply agreement with Australian partner
data.description:
• Allied Corp (OTCQB:ALID) announced, on Tuesday, the signing of a new supply agreement with an Australian-based medicinal cannabis company. The agreement will see Allied supplying Colombian-grown cannabis flower, further expanding its presence in Australia.
  • Allied’s partner has placed an initial purchase order for 50 kilograms of smokeable cannabis flower, with import permits already granted by the Australian regulators and delivery expected in early Q1, 2025.
  • The agreement is part of a Master Agreement setting terms for additional sales, with a follow-up order of 200 kilograms anticipated in H1 2025, subject to initial product meeting specifications.

data.title: Will a Santa Claus rally boost Wall Street this December? Here's what BofA predicts
data.description:
Investors look to position themselves to take advantage of what many hope to be the start of the Santa Claus rally that takes place in the market over the course of December.

“The Santa Claus rally is real and tends to occur over the last five sessions of December and the first two days of January,” Bank of America’s global research team stated in an investor note.

Looking through history and BofA showcased that dating back to 1928 data it shows that the month of December is likely to trade higher 74% of the time and the month made advancements with an average return of 1.32%.

Furthermore, regarding election years in December markets statistically were higher 83% of the time with an average return of 1.51%.

However, when looking closer at the idea of a Santa Claus rally, the data points to a backend-loaded month of returns compared to the earlier part of the month.

“December shows stronger SPX returns over the last ten sessions of the month vs the first ten days of the month. The first ten days of December have a lackluster SPX average return of 0.05% with this period up 59% of the time. The last ten sessions of December are much stronger with the SPX up 72% of the time on an average return of 1.17%,” BofA stated.

See the below seasonality chart that the BofA research team highlighted:

For market participants wanting to monitor the broader S&P 500 (SP500) more closely, here are some exchange traded funds and mutual funds that can serve as a proxy investment: (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (SSO), (RSP), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), and (VFFSX).

data.title: Kroger bull vs bear: Jefferies remains positive but BMO downgrades to Market Perform
data.description:
Two of the top international research firms have contrasting views on food and drug retailer Kroger (NYSE:KR).

Jefferies has upgraded the stock to Buy citing improving foot traffic, and fuel profitability, which has also led the investment bank to raise base EPS above Street. BMO Capital Markets, on the other hand, downgraded KR (KR) shares from Outperform to Market Perform; the $60 target price remains unchanged.

Jefferies says Kroger's fuel business, sometimes overlooked, has become structurally more profitable, providing incremental dollars for core grocery reinvestment.

It estimates KR will finish 2024 at ~14.6% fuel margin (vs. cons ~12.6%), a notable step-up from ~13.8% in '23 / ~10.5% avg from '14-'22. It also raises stand-alone base EPS to ~4%/4%/2% above cons for F'25/'26/'27.

Furthermore, it added that Albertsons' (ACI) operating inefficiency gives Kroger (KR) an opportunity to realize more synergies or become a stronger player with a pro-forma cash unlock to better compete with the likes of Walmart (WMT), Costco (COST), and Amazon (AMZN).

Jefferies forecasts a path to strong FCF accretion in F'26-'28, and added that inventory management alone is a material cash unlock.

BMO meanwhile believes KR remains on track to reiterate EPS targets for F25, but valuation expansion beyond 13x would likely require stronger grocery market share trends and/or upward EPS revisions.

For now, it trims F25E EPS slightly but remains at the mid-point of F25 guidance. While the pending ACI-KR merger still lacks clarity, the brokerage estimate that the potential EPS accretion from a deal or no-deal scenario is now largely reflected in the stock.

BMO however added that while KR's positioning in the industry may allow it to continue managing the competitive environment with stable GM%, particularly as the company "is turning the corner with incremental digital margins," it sees stock as fairly valued.

Seeking Alpha analysts have a Buy rating on the company.
